urn:contentItem:6JH3-HP93-S4TB-X1NK-00000-00TENASKA - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING SHELL PLC AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:58:58ZLexisNexisSTOCKTENASKA - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING SHELL PLC AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: TENASKA

PermID4296702736Websitehttp://www.tenaska.com/abouttenaska/IndustryOil And Gas

ACTIVITIES:

Tenaska, founded in 1987 in Omaha, Nebraska, is a leading independent energy company. With $4 billion in revenue and 800 employees, it operates across the energy value chain, including natural gas marketing (Tenaska Marketing Ventures), power marketing, and generation. Managing 7,482 MW of natural gas, wind, and solar facilities, Tenaskas portfolio includes development of 41,500 MW of renewable projects and 10 carbon sequestration initiatives. Its regional offices in Dallas, Denver, and Calgary support its North American operations. Tenaskas innovative solutions, like energy risk management, optimize value for clients. Consistently ranked among Forbes largest private companies, Tenaskas adaptability and expertise drive its leadership in the evolving energy sector.

+ TENASKA [3 News That Matters]

+ BOBWHITE ENERGY STORAGE PROJECT TO ADD 225 MW CAPACITY NEAR PHIPPS BEND NUCLEAR PLANT

Tenaska, with TVA, plans to invest $300 million in the Bobwhite Energy Storage Project at the Phipps Bend Industrial Park in Hawkins County, announced on 29th April 2026.Keith Latham, who leads Tenaska's battery storage facilities development, said the system operates much like a cellphone battery, charging overnight to discharge during peak usage.The venture is supported by a 20-year agreement that will not impose costs on Hawkins County. Rebecca Baker, executive administrative coordinator of the Hawkins County Industrial Development Board, noted that Tenaska will cover all property costs and invest substantially in the community, generating about $1 million in annual tax revenue.Jamie Wagner from Tenaska's community relations team added that the battery technology does not pose environmental risks.Construction is expected to begin in 2027 and the energy storage system is projected to be operational by 2029.

+ TVA SIGNS 225 MW BOBWHITE BATTERY CONTRACT WITH TENASKA FOR HAWKINS COUNTY

Bobwhite Energy Storage, a Tenaska affiliate, has signed a 20-year agreement with TVA for a 225 MW battery storage system in Hawkins County, East Tennessee, with the deal finalized on the 21st of April 2026. The arrangement strengthens TVA's ability to improve grid reliability and manage peak demand across its seven-state service territory.This contract lifts TVA's standalone battery storage commitments to 425 MW across its service region, reflecting growing demand for storage as the grid incorporates more resources.Monika Beckner, TVA Vice President of Power Supply & Fuels, said Bobwhite will bolster the Valley's energy security and improve its capacity to manage extreme conditions.John Hejkal, Tenaska's Vice President of Origination & Development, described Bobwhite as a clear example of the company's strategy to develop high-value projects that address grid challenges and market demand.Tenaska's track record includes about 14,000 MW of gas-fueled and renewable power developed, with a development portfolio totaling more than 33,000 MW, including 7,000 MW of battery storage. The Bobwhite project carries an estimated investment of roughly $300 million and is expected to create up to 75 construction jobs in Hawkins County, with opportunities for local suppliers and services that support the regional economy.

+ FLUVANNA BOARD APPROVES TENASKA PROJECT, OVERRIDING PLANNING COMMISSION ON PLAN AND HEIGHT

The Fluvanna County Board of Supervisors approved Tenaska's Expedition Generation project by a 4-1 vote, a move that overturned the Planning Commission's earlier finding that the project was not in substantial accord with the county Comprehensive Plan and authorized a zoning amendment to raise smokestack height. The action marks a pivotal step after months of debate.The approval followed a lengthy meeting that stretched to the early hours of 19th March 2026, with a 90-minute public comment period underscoring the community's division. Proponents argued the project aligns with the county's growth strategy and economic goals, while opponents raised concerns about health and air quality and the impact on rural character.Tenaska offered concessions, including stricter noise and lighting standards and a $5 million Good Neighbor Fund to aid residents. With local approvals in place, the company will advance to the state permitting phase, a process expected to take about two years before construction can begin, potentially allowing an opening as early as 2031.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SHELL [3 News That Matters]

+ SHELL PLC (L:SHEL) SETS $13.6 BILLION ARC RESOURCES DEAL TO BOOST LNG OUTPUT

Shell PLC (L:SHEL) Posts a strategic ARC Resources Ltd (TSX:ARX) acquisition valued at approximately $13.6 billion on 29th of April 2026. The deal is expected to lift Shell's oil and gas production through 2030 and expand its LNG capabilities.The transaction is valued at about $13.6 billion, with market sources indicating a near $14 billion figure. The agreement will boost LNG output by leveraging ARC's assets, strengthening Shell's LNG capacity and expanding access to diversified supply for Asia and Europe.The Strait of Hormuz disruption underscores the rationale to diversify supply chains and widen LNG sourcing to Asia and Europe. The move aligns with Shell's longer-term transition toward cleaner energy, with LNG seen as a cleaner-burning fuel to support sustainability goals

+ SHELL PLC (L:SHEL) ADVANCES $16 BILLION ARC RESOURCES ACQUISITION IN CASH-AND-STOCK STRUCTURE TO EXPAND CANADIAN OUTPUT

Shell PLC (L:SHEL) moves forward with a $16 billion cash-and-stock deal to acquire ARC Resources Ltd. as of 29th April 2026, seeking to strengthen its Canadian energy footprint. The transaction carries an equity value of about $13.6 billion and an enterprise value near $16.4 billion, including $2.8 billion of net debt and leases. ARC shareholders will receive 8.20 CAD in cash and 0.40247 Shell shares per ARC share, for a total of 32.80 CAD per ARC share and a 20% premium to ARC’s 30-day VWAP. Shell will finance the purchase with $3.4 billion in cash and $10.2 billion in Shell stock, issuing approximately 228 million new Shell shares. The deal has been unanimously approved by both boards and is targeted to close in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals. The investment aims to lift Shell’s daily production by about 370,000 boe/d, supporting a 4% growth trajectory through 2030 from 2025 levels. The combination of ARC’s more than 1.5 million net acres with Shell’s 440,000 acres expands the Montney basin footprint and strengthens Shell’s resource base while pursuing lower emissions. CEO Wael Sawan says the move aligns with Shell’s strategy to enhance its resource base and deliver growth with a focus on emissions management. Shell forecasts double-digit returns and accretive free cash flow per share from 2027, envisages roughly $250 million in annual synergies within a year of closing, and adds about 2 billion boe of proved and probable reserves to support LNG growth, including its stake in LNG Canada. The company keeps its cash capex outlook at $20–$22 billion for 2027 and 2028, and maintains a policy of distributing 40%–50% of cash flow from operations via dividends and buybacks.

+ SHELL PLC (NYSE:SHEL) SETS PATH TO LNG LEADERSHIP WITH ARC RESOURCES DEAL

Shell PLC (NYSE:SHEL) Posts ARC Resources Ltd. Acquisition to Bolster Upstream Output and LNG Position. The move will merge ARC's asset base with Shell's upstream portfolio to lift output and reserves, while strengthening Shell's LNG capabilities globally. The transaction follows Shell's BG Group merger as a landmark step in its strategy. It is slated to take effect after negotiations are finalized, a process accelerated by evolving LNG-market dynamics and geopolitics.Shell confirmed on 29th April 2026 the ARC Resources deal and described it as value-accretive. ARC Resources shareholders will receive a substantial premium, and ARC investors will gain a discounted entry into Shell's shareholder base. In addition, the combination will deliver greater scale in LNG and improved competitiveness for Shell in a market shaped by energy-transition pressures and supply concerns.Shell's leadership expects the acquisition to position the company as a leading LNG player with a stronger upstream platform, leveraging ARC's resource base and Shell's integrated gas capabilities. The move underscores the company's strategy to capture value in LNG growth markets, particularly in regions where supply volatility has increased.Analysts have upgraded Shell's stock from hold to buy, citing the stronger LNG outlook and potential for extended long-term value. Investors will monitor integration progress and any incremental synergies that could lift volumes, cash flow, and returns.For ARC Resources shareholders, holding their shares through the merger remains a recommended path to maximize the premium and transition into Shell's long-term growth trajectory.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER [3 News That Matters]

+ AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER COMPANY, INC. (NASDAQ:AEP) SETS GROWTH PATH WITH EXPANDED SHARE AUTHORIZATION AND 72 BILLION CAPITAL PLAN

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) Posts results from its 119th annual meeting, where shareholders re-elected the full 10-director slate and ratified PwC as the independent auditor for FY 2026. The meeting, conducted virtually on April 28, 2026, also approved increasing authorized common shares from 600 million to 900 million to underpin future capital deploymentFehrman reports 2025 operating earnings of $5.97 per share, a 25% jump in the company’s share price, and a 29% total shareholder return. He reiterates a 7% to 9% long-term growth target and highlights strategic investments in grid and generation to support anticipated AI-driven load growthSara Martinez Tucker, the lead director, states the board remains committed to delivering reliable, affordable power across AEP’s 11-state regulated footprint. The board’s approval of a substantial $72 billion capital plan underscores disciplined investment in core areas—generation, transmission, and distribution—while residential rate increases are targeted to be capped at 3.5% annuallyThe company also approved an employee stock purchase plan and received an advisory vote on executive compensation. Shareholders asked no notable questions, and AEP intends to file final voting results with the SEC

+ AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER COMPANY, INC. (NASDAQ:AEP) SETS QUARTERLY DIVIDEND AT $0.95 PER SHARE

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) Posts A Quarterly Dividend Of $0.95 Per Share, Maintaining A 2.8% Forward Yield. The company disclosed on Apr. 28, 2026 that the payout remains $0.95 per share for the coming quarter and that the dividend will be paid on Jun. 10 to shareholders of record on May 8, the ex-dividend date. The dividend is supported by a diverse portfolio and stands as part of the company's ongoing capital return strategy. The utility operates as a large regulated utility, serving more than 5 million customers across 11 states and maintains a market capitalization near $73.64 billion. It carries a diversified energy mix including coal, natural gas, renewables, and nuclear, underscoring its role in the U.S. energy landscape. From a valuation perspective, the stock trades at a price-to-earnings ratio of 20.31x, near the high end of its historical range. Insiders sold about $0.70 million of shares in recent months, with no reported insider purchases. The GF Score remains at 80/100, while GF Value data is unavailable. Investors should weigh dividend stability against regulatory exposure and the company's growth prospects when evaluating the stock

+ AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER COMPANY, INC. (NASDAQ:AEP) FILES TO EXPAND GENERATION AS DATA CENTER DEMAND RISES

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) said on 13th April 2026 that it will file for additional generation capacity to meet rising energy demands, led by Indiana Michigan Power (I&M). The plan aims to ensure reliable power for existing customers and to support anticipated growth from the data center boom and other drivers.The filing signals AEP's intent to expand its generation portfolio in response to evolving energy use patterns and to attract new investments in the region.AEP cites the data center boom as a key contributor to demand, with cloud computing and digital services increasing the need for robust energy supply.In addition to capacity expansion, the company emphasizes sustainability and governance, noting efforts to maintain a strong MSCI ESG score as part of its long term viability.Investors and stakeholders will monitor the plan as AEP balances expansion with reliability and ESG commitments, as it seeks to attract future investments in the region.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CONOCOPHILLIPS [3 News That Matters]

+ CONOCOPHILLIPS (NYSE:COP) ALIGNS NORTH SLOPE PROJECTS UNDER UNIFIED OPERATIONAL PLATFORM

ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) is expanding its strategic planning for North Slope development, outlining a move toward an integrated platform that supports predictability and environmental stewardship. On 14th April 2026, the company said the planning approach now emphasizes a cohesive framework across projects. The Western North Slope's fields, processing facilities and infrastructure are highly interconnected, requiring a planning method that accounts for cross project dependencies, logistics and community engagement. ConocoPhillips aims to map these relationships to avoid bottlenecks and reduce wasteful duplication. The company is pursuing integrated planning across project areas, treating developments as elements of a larger family of initiatives to identify synergies such as shared logistics corridors and infrastructure reuse. This strategy is designed to streamline operations while respecting environmental constraints in the region. Management notes that a conservative pace may reflect a deliberate optimization of multiple projects, not inertia. By establishing a common operational platform, ConocoPhillips seeks to minimize strain on logistics systems and the workforce. The evolving method could redefine Arctic development practices and influence future projects in Alaska and beyond.

+ CONOCOPHILLIPS (NYSE:COP) EVALUATES IN COUNTRY VENEZUELA PROJECTS AFTER POLITICAL SHIFTS

ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) has dispatched a team to Venezuela to evaluate potential oil and gas opportunities, confirming the move on 13th April 2026. The company aims to gauge viability of investments in the region and will compare Venezuela’s prospects against other international opportunities as part of its disciplined investment framework. The team was deployed during the week of 6th April 2026 to gather on-site insights into in-country projects.ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) previously exited Venezuela in 2007, after the nationalization of oil fields under then-President Hugo Chavez. The company, alongside ExxonMobil, contested the terms and sought redress through the World Bank’s International Centre for the Settlement of Investment Disputes, seeking approximately $12 billion. Prior to its exit, ConocoPhillips held significant stakes in multiple Venezuelan heavy oil ventures.The geopolitical landscape shifted with the removal of Nicolas Maduro, prompting U.S. President Donald Trump to urge oil companies to invest in rebuilding Venezuela's energy sector. ExxonMobil has similarly reevaluated its position, sending a technical team in March to explore opportunities.At CERAWeek by S&P Global in Houston, ConocoPhillips CEO Ryan Lance stressed that Venezuela must overhaul its fiscal system to attract fresh investments. The country still holds vast proven oil reserves but faces production setbacks tied to years of mismanagement, underinvestment and sanctions.

+ CONOCOPHILLIPS SIGNALS ENHANCED SHAREHOLDER VALUE THROUGH DISTRIBUTIONS

ConocoPhillips is boosting shareholder value as the oil market remains robust. As of 31st March 2026, the company's emphasis on distributions and capital allocation aligns with strong sector dynamics, reinforcing its role in the upstream oil landscape.The recent rise in crude prices, driven by geopolitical factors, has translated into higher operational inflows for ConocoPhillips. This environment allows the company to capitalize on its diverse asset base, which includes conventional oil fields and LNG projects, while geographic diversification helps manage supply disruptions across its North American and international operations.Management has reiterated a commitment to distributing excess financial resources to shareholders, prioritizing dividends to align with peers that emphasize returns.The approach also seeks to balance reinvestment in large-scale opportunities with immediate distributions, reflecting a disciplined capital allocation strategy designed to sustain operations while rewarding investors.Looking forward, ConocoPhillips plans to maintain dividend growth and continue expanding LNG projects, supporting energy transition trends and diversifying revenue as it navigates evolving demand and geopolitical dynamics, with efficiency and value creation remaining central to its market position.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DOMINION ENERGY [3 News That Matters]

+ DOMINION ENERGY, INC. (NYSE:D) REPORTS $1,270,000 IN LOBBYING OUTLAYS FOR FIRST QUARTER 2026

Dominion Energy, Inc. (NYSE:D) Posts $1,270,000 in lobbying expenditures for the first quarter of 2026, per a Lobbying Disclosure Act filing dated 21st April 2026.The filing outlines engagement on natural gas and electric utility issues, nuclear energy considerations, and offshore wind projects, including the Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind Project and energy modernization for military installations.Regulatory matters include permitting reforms, environmental regulations, and cybersecurity issues, with a focus on policies for small modular reactors and renewable natural gas as part of a broader generation and resilience strategy.The National Defense Authorization Act context is referenced, with provisions touching on utilities privatization and energy resilience in military settings.Institutional investor activity is notable, with 639 institutions increasing stakes and 626 reducing holdings, according to Quiver Quantitative. Wellington Management Group added over 14 million shares, while UBS Asset Management reduced its stake by nearly 75%.Analysts have issued overweight ratings, with price targets ranging from $64.00 to $67.00, highlighting continued optimism about the stock despite policy and market complexities.

+ DOMINION ENERGY EXTENDS REVOLVING CREDIT AGREEMENTS, LENGTHENING DEBT MATURING SCHEDULE

Dominion Energy said in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Apr. 10, 2026 that it has amended two revolving credit agreements to extend their maturities. One amendment extends the Sustainability Revolving Credit Agreement to Apr. 7, 2029, with the option to add up to two one-year extensions, subject to specified conditions. The other amendment, agreed with Virginia Electric and Power Company and Dominion Energy South Carolina, Inc., extends the Sixth Amended and Restated Revolving Credit Agreement to Apr. 8, 2031, with JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. serving as administrative agent. Dominion Energy also notes a $50.2 billion total debt load and a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.79 as it pursues a longer-financing horizon. The company trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker D, and its Q4 2025 results showed EPS of $0.68 on revenue of $4.09 billion, a mixed reading for investors.

+ HENRICO'S SOLAR ACCESS PROGRAM PROVIDES NO-COST INSTALLATIONS TO LOWER HOME POWER BILLS

Residents in Henrico County are being offered a no-cost pathway to solar power through the Solar Access Henrico program, a collaboration between Dominion Energy and the county that began in April 2025. A $5 million funding package from the White Oak Technology Park rezoning supports 250 installations, with capacity remaining for at least 100 more homes.Dominion Energy emphasizes that the program removes the biggest hurdle to solar adoption—the upfront cost. Typical solar setups can cost $20,000-$30,000, but participants in Solar Access Henrico will not pay for the installation. Participants are projected to save as much as 50% to 95% on their electricity bills, depending on seasonal usage and sun exposure. Installations are designed to be completed in a half day to a full day, and early feedback has been favorable.Feedback from residents who have joined the program has been positive. Deborah Stroud, a Henrico resident, noted that while her solar setup helps, winter sunlight can limit savings. She also pointed out that in summer her energy bills have sometimes fallen to under $100, and on some months she has had no electric bill at all due to solar.Officials caution that winter savings may be less pronounced, but anticipate substantial reductions in spring and summer. Not all homes qualify—ideal candidates include south-facing rooftops and roofs in good condition, given that solar panels typically last 25 to 30 years. Additional details and eligibility criteria are available on Dominion Energy’s website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BP [3 News That Matters]

+ BP PLC (L:BP.) PARTNERS WITH VENEZUELAN GOVERNMENT ON OFFSHORE LORAN GAS EXPLORATION DEAL

BP PLC (L:BP.) Partners with the Venezuelan government to explore the offshore Loran gas area, in a deal dated 29th April 2026 at 14:49 EDT, underscoring BP's push to broaden its regional portfolio. The Loran field is believed to hold significant reserves that could supply both local and international markets. The project supports BP's strategy to diversify its energy mix away from crude oil while pursuing cleaner energy solutions. The partnership leverages local Venezuelan expertise and resources, but BP remains exposed to political and economic volatility in the country. Investors will monitor the venture as BP advances its broader transition to cleaner energy and assesses how non-OPEC supply trends may influence the gas outlook.

+ BP PLC (L:BP.) REAFFIRMS VENEZUELA ENGAGEMENT THROUGH COCUINA-MANAKIN DEVELOPMENT AND LORAN PROSPECTS

CARACAS, April 29 BP plc (L:BP.) Reports signing a memorandum of understanding with the Venezuelan government on April 29, 2026 to develop the Cocuina-Manakin gas field and to explore opportunities in the offshore Loran gas field, signaling a renewed energy partnership. Venezuela has been actively courting foreign investment in its energy sector amid political changes this year, with interim President Delcy Rodriguez describing BP’s return as a pivotal moment for the country’s energy landscape and stressing the benefits of respectful, mutually advantageous collaboration for the Venezuelan people.

+ BP PLC (L:BP.) AZULE ENERGY REPORTS OIL DISCOVERY OFF ANGOLA IN BLOCK 15/06

BP PLC (L:BP.) Azule Energy Reports Oil Discovery Off Angola in Block 15/06. The Algaita-01 exploration well, located in the Lower Congo Basin, is estimated to hold nearly 500 million barrels of crude oil, according to a statement from Angola's National Oil, Gas, and Biofuels Agency (ANPG) on 26 Apr 2026. The discovery occurs within Azule Energy's ongoing campaign that has yielded 22 discoveries in Block 15/06 to date, highlighting the area's petroleum system. Drilling began January 10, 2026 at a water depth of 667 meters, approximately 18 kilometers from the Olombendo FPSO, and was completed on January 26. The Algaita-01 find reinforces BP's focus on expanding its African footprint, and adds to Azule Energy's portfolio in a region that ANPG has described as showing consistent exploration activity. Joe Murphy, CEO of Azule Energy, stated that the results reaffirm the high potential of the Lower Congo Basin. Paulino Jeronimo, Chairman & CEO of ANPG, emphasized the consistency of exploration in the area, underscoring support for continued activity in offshore Angola

urn:contentItem:6JGB-XYT3-SH69-R3JY-00000-00The Rewind: May 12026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:51:17ZLexisNexisREWINDThe Rewind: May 1Staff Writer

The BLK Squirrel celebrating 10 years of good drinks, community on the 100 Block

The BLK Squirrel bar, located at 154 W. Broadway in Council Bluffs, offers a diverse range of drinks from Busch Light to fine bourbon. Owned by Andy Mead and Nathan Verzal, the bar has been a neighborhood staple since 2016. The space features a welcoming atmosphere with exposed brick and vintage decor, aiming to be a comfortable spot for all visitors.

Mead and Verzal, experienced bartenders, also operate The Green Room in Omaha. They emphasize community and cooperation among local businesses, contributing to the vibrant 100 Block district. The bar is open daily, providing a space where diverse clientele can connect over drinks and conversation.

Read full article →

Mary Ann Hanusa may draw controversy just not for her role in politics

Mary Ann Hanusa, currently the Pottawattamie County auditor, has a long history in public service, including roles in the National Archives and under Presidents Reagan and Bush. She also managed Sen. Chuck Grassley's campaign and served in the Iowa House. Hanusa, passionate about history, emphasizes its narrative aspect over memorization.

Raised in Council Bluffs, Hanusa credits her parents and history teachers for inspiring her career. Outside of work, she enjoys cooking and baking, viewing it as therapeutic. Hanusa, who listens to Christmas music year-round, aims to be remembered for her kindness and convictions.

Read full article →

Jeff Jorgensen faces reelection for first time since arrest in 2024 and grief counseling

Jeff Jorgensen seeks a second term on the Pottawattamie County Board of Supervisors, despite a 2024 arrest for driving while intoxicated. He attributed the incident to grief over his wife's death, completed a diversion program, and paid a fine. Jorgensen aims to foster county growth and address tax issues, emphasizing collaboration with cities like Council Bluffs and Omaha.

Jorgensen opposes new windmills due to local sentiment and advocates for selling or leasing the county-owned Crescent Hill ski hill. He stresses the board's need to manage budgets effectively, especially concerning infrastructure and salaries, while remaining open to community input.

Read full article →

Charlie Johnson hopes to join Board of Supervisors after falling short in last election

Charlie Johnson, a Republican, is running for the Pottawattamie County Board of Supervisors after previous unsuccessful attempts. Following Scott Belt's removal, Johnson was not appointed last year, but he has since focused on understanding county operations. He aims to bring stable leadership, accountability, and transparency to the board, emphasizing listening to residents and fostering cooperation with local communities.

Johnson identifies taxes and budget management as major concerns, advocating for innovative solutions to reduce taxpayer burdens. He supports collaboration with cities and prioritizes infrastructure improvements and renewable energy regulation, while respecting farmers' rights. Johnson's diverse experience in public, private and nonprofit sectors bolsters his candidacy.

Read full article →

value.Title: TENASKA - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM SHELL PLC AND ITS PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGF-M5B3-RS42-W4HW-00000-00TENASKA - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM SHELL PLC AND ITS PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:58:58ZLexisNexisSTOCKTENASKA - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM SHELL PLC AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: TENASKA

PermID4296702736Websitehttp://www.tenaska.com/abouttenaska/IndustryOil And Gas

ACTIVITIES:

Tenaska, founded in 1987 in Omaha, Nebraska, is a leading independent energy company. With $4 billion in revenue and 800 employees, it operates across the energy value chain, including natural gas marketing (Tenaska Marketing Ventures), power marketing, and generation. Managing 7,482 MW of natural gas, wind, and solar facilities, Tenaskas portfolio includes development of 41,500 MW of renewable projects and 10 carbon sequestration initiatives. Its regional offices in Dallas, Denver, and Calgary support its North American operations. Tenaskas innovative solutions, like energy risk management, optimize value for clients. Consistently ranked among Forbes largest private companies, Tenaskas adaptability and expertise drive its leadership in the evolving energy sector.

+ TENASKA [3 News That Matters]

+ TVA SIGNS 225 MW BOBWHITE BATTERY CONTRACT WITH TENASKA FOR HAWKINS COUNTY

Bobwhite Energy Storage, a Tenaska affiliate, has signed a 20-year agreement with TVA for a 225 MW battery storage system in Hawkins County, East Tennessee, with the deal finalized on the 21st of April 2026. The arrangement strengthens TVA's ability to improve grid reliability and manage peak demand across its seven-state service territory.This contract lifts TVA's standalone battery storage commitments to 425 MW across its service region, reflecting growing demand for storage as the grid incorporates more resources.Monika Beckner, TVA Vice President of Power Supply & Fuels, said Bobwhite will bolster the Valley's energy security and improve its capacity to manage extreme conditions.John Hejkal, Tenaska's Vice President of Origination & Development, described Bobwhite as a clear example of the company's strategy to develop high-value projects that address grid challenges and market demand.Tenaska's track record includes about 14,000 MW of gas-fueled and renewable power developed, with a development portfolio totaling more than 33,000 MW, including 7,000 MW of battery storage. The Bobwhite project carries an estimated investment of roughly $300 million and is expected to create up to 75 construction jobs in Hawkins County, with opportunities for local suppliers and services that support the regional economy.

+ FLUVANNA BOARD APPROVES TENASKA PROJECT, OVERRIDING PLANNING COMMISSION ON PLAN AND HEIGHT

The Fluvanna County Board of Supervisors approved Tenaska's Expedition Generation project by a 4-1 vote, a move that overturned the Planning Commission's earlier finding that the project was not in substantial accord with the county Comprehensive Plan and authorized a zoning amendment to raise smokestack height. The action marks a pivotal step after months of debate.The approval followed a lengthy meeting that stretched to the early hours of 19th March 2026, with a 90-minute public comment period underscoring the community's division. Proponents argued the project aligns with the county's growth strategy and economic goals, while opponents raised concerns about health and air quality and the impact on rural character.Tenaska offered concessions, including stricter noise and lighting standards and a $5 million Good Neighbor Fund to aid residents. With local approvals in place, the company will advance to the state permitting phase, a process expected to take about two years before construction can begin, potentially allowing an opening as early as 2031.

+ COMMUNITY MEETING HIGHLIGHTS TENSIONS OVER TENASKA AND INFRASTRUCTURE NEEDS

The town hall on 12th March 2026, hosted by Rivanna District Supervisor Tony O’Brien and Palmyra District Supervisor Tim Hodge, drew a large crowd in Fluvanna County as residents raised questions about Aqua water rates, the Tenaska natural gas power plant, and the county’s long-term infrastructure plans.The meeting aimed to be informal, but much of the discussion centered on escalating infrastructure needs and the Tenaska project, with residents asking about the county’s budget process and development proposals.O’Brien underscored water service upgrades in response to Aqua Virginia rate hikes, noting two increases of roughly 30 percent in 18 months and contrasting these with typical state adjustments.The Tenaska proposal prompted debate over air quality, health impacts, and whether the county had conducted independent analysis; some pointed to approximately $250 million in projected tax revenue, while others warned that environmental risks could offset the economic benefits.As the debate continued, O’Brien reassured attendees that the Board would consider all perspectives before a final decision on the project’s special use permits, highlighting the challenge of balancing economic development with community health and environmental sustainability in Fluvanna County.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SHELL [3 News That Matters]

+ SHELL PLC (L:SHEL) ADVANCES $16 BILLION ARC RESOURCES ACQUISITION IN CASH-AND-STOCK STRUCTURE TO EXPAND CANADIAN OUTPUT

Shell PLC (L:SHEL) moves forward with a $16 billion cash-and-stock deal to acquire ARC Resources Ltd. as of 29th April 2026, seeking to strengthen its Canadian energy footprint. The transaction carries an equity value of about $13.6 billion and an enterprise value near $16.4 billion, including $2.8 billion of net debt and leases. ARC shareholders will receive 8.20 CAD in cash and 0.40247 Shell shares per ARC share, for a total of 32.80 CAD per ARC share and a 20% premium to ARC’s 30-day VWAP. Shell will finance the purchase with $3.4 billion in cash and $10.2 billion in Shell stock, issuing approximately 228 million new Shell shares. The deal has been unanimously approved by both boards and is targeted to close in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals. The investment aims to lift Shell’s daily production by about 370,000 boe/d, supporting a 4% growth trajectory through 2030 from 2025 levels. The combination of ARC’s more than 1.5 million net acres with Shell’s 440,000 acres expands the Montney basin footprint and strengthens Shell’s resource base while pursuing lower emissions. CEO Wael Sawan says the move aligns with Shell’s strategy to enhance its resource base and deliver growth with a focus on emissions management. Shell forecasts double-digit returns and accretive free cash flow per share from 2027, envisages roughly $250 million in annual synergies within a year of closing, and adds about 2 billion boe of proved and probable reserves to support LNG growth, including its stake in LNG Canada. The company keeps its cash capex outlook at $20–$22 billion for 2027 and 2028, and maintains a policy of distributing 40%–50% of cash flow from operations via dividends and buybacks.

+ SHELL PLC (L:SHEL) SETS $13.6 BILLION ARC RESOURCES DEAL TO BOOST LNG OUTPUT

Shell PLC (L:SHEL) Posts a strategic ARC Resources Ltd (TSX:ARX) acquisition valued at approximately $13.6 billion on 29th of April 2026. The deal is expected to lift Shell's oil and gas production through 2030 and expand its LNG capabilities.The transaction is valued at about $13.6 billion, with market sources indicating a near $14 billion figure. The agreement will boost LNG output by leveraging ARC's assets, strengthening Shell's LNG capacity and expanding access to diversified supply for Asia and Europe.The Strait of Hormuz disruption underscores the rationale to diversify supply chains and widen LNG sourcing to Asia and Europe. The move aligns with Shell's longer-term transition toward cleaner energy, with LNG seen as a cleaner-burning fuel to support sustainability goals

+ SHELL PLC (NYSE:SHEL) SETS PATH TO LNG LEADERSHIP WITH ARC RESOURCES DEAL

Shell PLC (NYSE:SHEL) Posts ARC Resources Ltd. Acquisition to Bolster Upstream Output and LNG Position. The move will merge ARC's asset base with Shell's upstream portfolio to lift output and reserves, while strengthening Shell's LNG capabilities globally. The transaction follows Shell's BG Group merger as a landmark step in its strategy. It is slated to take effect after negotiations are finalized, a process accelerated by evolving LNG-market dynamics and geopolitics.Shell confirmed on 29th April 2026 the ARC Resources deal and described it as value-accretive. ARC Resources shareholders will receive a substantial premium, and ARC investors will gain a discounted entry into Shell's shareholder base. In addition, the combination will deliver greater scale in LNG and improved competitiveness for Shell in a market shaped by energy-transition pressures and supply concerns.Shell's leadership expects the acquisition to position the company as a leading LNG player with a stronger upstream platform, leveraging ARC's resource base and Shell's integrated gas capabilities. The move underscores the company's strategy to capture value in LNG growth markets, particularly in regions where supply volatility has increased.Analysts have upgraded Shell's stock from hold to buy, citing the stronger LNG outlook and potential for extended long-term value. Investors will monitor integration progress and any incremental synergies that could lift volumes, cash flow, and returns.For ARC Resources shareholders, holding their shares through the merger remains a recommended path to maximize the premium and transition into Shell's long-term growth trajectory.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER [3 News That Matters]

+ AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER COMPANY, INC. (NASDAQ:AEP) SETS GROWTH PATH WITH EXPANDED SHARE AUTHORIZATION AND 72 BILLION CAPITAL PLAN

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) Posts results from its 119th annual meeting, where shareholders re-elected the full 10-director slate and ratified PwC as the independent auditor for FY 2026. The meeting, conducted virtually on April 28, 2026, also approved increasing authorized common shares from 600 million to 900 million to underpin future capital deploymentFehrman reports 2025 operating earnings of $5.97 per share, a 25% jump in the company’s share price, and a 29% total shareholder return. He reiterates a 7% to 9% long-term growth target and highlights strategic investments in grid and generation to support anticipated AI-driven load growthSara Martinez Tucker, the lead director, states the board remains committed to delivering reliable, affordable power across AEP’s 11-state regulated footprint. The board’s approval of a substantial $72 billion capital plan underscores disciplined investment in core areas—generation, transmission, and distribution—while residential rate increases are targeted to be capped at 3.5% annuallyThe company also approved an employee stock purchase plan and received an advisory vote on executive compensation. Shareholders asked no notable questions, and AEP intends to file final voting results with the SEC

+ AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER COMPANY, INC. (NASDAQ:AEP) SETS QUARTERLY DIVIDEND AT $0.95 PER SHARE

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) Posts A Quarterly Dividend Of $0.95 Per Share, Maintaining A 2.8% Forward Yield. The company disclosed on Apr. 28, 2026 that the payout remains $0.95 per share for the coming quarter and that the dividend will be paid on Jun. 10 to shareholders of record on May 8, the ex-dividend date. The dividend is supported by a diverse portfolio and stands as part of the company's ongoing capital return strategy. The utility operates as a large regulated utility, serving more than 5 million customers across 11 states and maintains a market capitalization near $73.64 billion. It carries a diversified energy mix including coal, natural gas, renewables, and nuclear, underscoring its role in the U.S. energy landscape. From a valuation perspective, the stock trades at a price-to-earnings ratio of 20.31x, near the high end of its historical range. Insiders sold about $0.70 million of shares in recent months, with no reported insider purchases. The GF Score remains at 80/100, while GF Value data is unavailable. Investors should weigh dividend stability against regulatory exposure and the company's growth prospects when evaluating the stock

+ AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER COMPANY, INC. (NASDAQ:AEP) NAMES MARYAM S. BROWN TO LEAD INDIANA MICHIGAN POWER

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (NASDAQ:AEP) Names Maryam S. Brown to Lead Indiana Michigan Power, effective 27th April 2026. Brown will serve as president and chief operating officer of Indiana Michigan Power and will report to Bill Fehrman, AEP chairman, president and chief executive officer.Brown brings more than 25 years of utility operations and regulatory experience, most recently leading Southern California Gas Company as president and chief executive officer, where she modernized operations and advanced the Angeles Link project, a proposed hydrogen pipeline that would be among the largest in the United States.She succeeds Steve Baker, who will move into a senior advisor role through the end of 2026 to support the handover and assist with AEP's nuclear development strategy in Indiana.Brown's early career included maintenance and reliability engineering at Amoco Oil, followed by a law degree. She has worked as a Securities and Exchange Commission attorney and as chief counsel for the House Energy and Power Subcommittee, before joining Sempra in 2016 and later leading SoCalGas.American Electric Power operates about 40,000 line miles of transmission and over 252,000 miles of distribution lines, serving 5.6 million customers across 11 states. The company plans to invest $72 billion from 2026 through 2030 and has maintained dividend payments for 56 consecutive years. The stock has traded near a 52-week high of $137.74, with a 34.62% return in the past year. Some analysts view the stock as overvalued relative to fair value.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CONOCOPHILLIPS [3 News That Matters]

+ CONOCOPHILLIPS (NYSE:COP) ALIGNS NORTH SLOPE PROJECTS UNDER UNIFIED OPERATIONAL PLATFORM

ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) is expanding its strategic planning for North Slope development, outlining a move toward an integrated platform that supports predictability and environmental stewardship. On 14th April 2026, the company said the planning approach now emphasizes a cohesive framework across projects. The Western North Slope's fields, processing facilities and infrastructure are highly interconnected, requiring a planning method that accounts for cross project dependencies, logistics and community engagement. ConocoPhillips aims to map these relationships to avoid bottlenecks and reduce wasteful duplication. The company is pursuing integrated planning across project areas, treating developments as elements of a larger family of initiatives to identify synergies such as shared logistics corridors and infrastructure reuse. This strategy is designed to streamline operations while respecting environmental constraints in the region. Management notes that a conservative pace may reflect a deliberate optimization of multiple projects, not inertia. By establishing a common operational platform, ConocoPhillips seeks to minimize strain on logistics systems and the workforce. The evolving method could redefine Arctic development practices and influence future projects in Alaska and beyond.

+ CONOCOPHILLIPS (NYSE:COP) EVALUATES IN COUNTRY VENEZUELA PROJECTS AFTER POLITICAL SHIFTS

ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) has dispatched a team to Venezuela to evaluate potential oil and gas opportunities, confirming the move on 13th April 2026. The company aims to gauge viability of investments in the region and will compare Venezuela’s prospects against other international opportunities as part of its disciplined investment framework. The team was deployed during the week of 6th April 2026 to gather on-site insights into in-country projects.ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) previously exited Venezuela in 2007, after the nationalization of oil fields under then-President Hugo Chavez. The company, alongside ExxonMobil, contested the terms and sought redress through the World Bank’s International Centre for the Settlement of Investment Disputes, seeking approximately $12 billion. Prior to its exit, ConocoPhillips held significant stakes in multiple Venezuelan heavy oil ventures.The geopolitical landscape shifted with the removal of Nicolas Maduro, prompting U.S. President Donald Trump to urge oil companies to invest in rebuilding Venezuela's energy sector. ExxonMobil has similarly reevaluated its position, sending a technical team in March to explore opportunities.At CERAWeek by S&P Global in Houston, ConocoPhillips CEO Ryan Lance stressed that Venezuela must overhaul its fiscal system to attract fresh investments. The country still holds vast proven oil reserves but faces production setbacks tied to years of mismanagement, underinvestment and sanctions.

+ CONOCOPHILLIPS SIGNALS ENHANCED SHAREHOLDER VALUE THROUGH DISTRIBUTIONS

ConocoPhillips is boosting shareholder value as the oil market remains robust. As of 31st March 2026, the company's emphasis on distributions and capital allocation aligns with strong sector dynamics, reinforcing its role in the upstream oil landscape.The recent rise in crude prices, driven by geopolitical factors, has translated into higher operational inflows for ConocoPhillips. This environment allows the company to capitalize on its diverse asset base, which includes conventional oil fields and LNG projects, while geographic diversification helps manage supply disruptions across its North American and international operations.Management has reiterated a commitment to distributing excess financial resources to shareholders, prioritizing dividends to align with peers that emphasize returns.The approach also seeks to balance reinvestment in large-scale opportunities with immediate distributions, reflecting a disciplined capital allocation strategy designed to sustain operations while rewarding investors.Looking forward, ConocoPhillips plans to maintain dividend growth and continue expanding LNG projects, supporting energy transition trends and diversifying revenue as it navigates evolving demand and geopolitical dynamics, with efficiency and value creation remaining central to its market position.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DOMINION ENERGY [3 News That Matters]

+ DOMINION ENERGY, INC. (NYSE:D) REPORTS $1,270,000 IN LOBBYING OUTLAYS FOR FIRST QUARTER 2026

Dominion Energy, Inc. (NYSE:D) Posts $1,270,000 in lobbying expenditures for the first quarter of 2026, per a Lobbying Disclosure Act filing dated 21st April 2026.The filing outlines engagement on natural gas and electric utility issues, nuclear energy considerations, and offshore wind projects, including the Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind Project and energy modernization for military installations.Regulatory matters include permitting reforms, environmental regulations, and cybersecurity issues, with a focus on policies for small modular reactors and renewable natural gas as part of a broader generation and resilience strategy.The National Defense Authorization Act context is referenced, with provisions touching on utilities privatization and energy resilience in military settings.Institutional investor activity is notable, with 639 institutions increasing stakes and 626 reducing holdings, according to Quiver Quantitative. Wellington Management Group added over 14 million shares, while UBS Asset Management reduced its stake by nearly 75%.Analysts have issued overweight ratings, with price targets ranging from $64.00 to $67.00, highlighting continued optimism about the stock despite policy and market complexities.

+ DOMINION ENERGY EXTENDS REVOLVING CREDIT AGREEMENTS, LENGTHENING DEBT MATURING SCHEDULE

Dominion Energy said in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Apr. 10, 2026 that it has amended two revolving credit agreements to extend their maturities. One amendment extends the Sustainability Revolving Credit Agreement to Apr. 7, 2029, with the option to add up to two one-year extensions, subject to specified conditions. The other amendment, agreed with Virginia Electric and Power Company and Dominion Energy South Carolina, Inc., extends the Sixth Amended and Restated Revolving Credit Agreement to Apr. 8, 2031, with JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. serving as administrative agent. Dominion Energy also notes a $50.2 billion total debt load and a debt-to-equity ratio of 1.79 as it pursues a longer-financing horizon. The company trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker D, and its Q4 2025 results showed EPS of $0.68 on revenue of $4.09 billion, a mixed reading for investors.

+ HENRICO'S SOLAR ACCESS PROGRAM PROVIDES NO-COST INSTALLATIONS TO LOWER HOME POWER BILLS

Residents in Henrico County are being offered a no-cost pathway to solar power through the Solar Access Henrico program, a collaboration between Dominion Energy and the county that began in April 2025. A $5 million funding package from the White Oak Technology Park rezoning supports 250 installations, with capacity remaining for at least 100 more homes.Dominion Energy emphasizes that the program removes the biggest hurdle to solar adoption—the upfront cost. Typical solar setups can cost $20,000-$30,000, but participants in Solar Access Henrico will not pay for the installation. Participants are projected to save as much as 50% to 95% on their electricity bills, depending on seasonal usage and sun exposure. Installations are designed to be completed in a half day to a full day, and early feedback has been favorable.Feedback from residents who have joined the program has been positive. Deborah Stroud, a Henrico resident, noted that while her solar setup helps, winter sunlight can limit savings. She also pointed out that in summer her energy bills have sometimes fallen to under $100, and on some months she has had no electric bill at all due to solar.Officials caution that winter savings may be less pronounced, but anticipate substantial reductions in spring and summer. Not all homes qualify—ideal candidates include south-facing rooftops and roofs in good condition, given that solar panels typically last 25 to 30 years. Additional details and eligibility criteria are available on Dominion Energy’s website.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BP [3 News That Matters]

+ BP PLC (L:BP.) REAFFIRMS VENEZUELA ENGAGEMENT THROUGH COCUINA-MANAKIN DEVELOPMENT AND LORAN PROSPECTS

CARACAS, April 29 BP plc (L:BP.) Reports signing a memorandum of understanding with the Venezuelan government on April 29, 2026 to develop the Cocuina-Manakin gas field and to explore opportunities in the offshore Loran gas field, signaling a renewed energy partnership. Venezuela has been actively courting foreign investment in its energy sector amid political changes this year, with interim President Delcy Rodriguez describing BP’s return as a pivotal moment for the country’s energy landscape and stressing the benefits of respectful, mutually advantageous collaboration for the Venezuelan people.

+ BP PLC (L:BP.) PARTNERS WITH VENEZUELAN GOVERNMENT ON OFFSHORE LORAN GAS EXPLORATION DEAL

BP PLC (L:BP.) Partners with the Venezuelan government to explore the offshore Loran gas area, in a deal dated 29th April 2026 at 14:49 EDT, underscoring BP's push to broaden its regional portfolio. The Loran field is believed to hold significant reserves that could supply both local and international markets. The project supports BP's strategy to diversify its energy mix away from crude oil while pursuing cleaner energy solutions. The partnership leverages local Venezuelan expertise and resources, but BP remains exposed to political and economic volatility in the country. Investors will monitor the venture as BP advances its broader transition to cleaner energy and assesses how non-OPEC supply trends may influence the gas outlook.

+ BP PLC (L:BP.) AZULE ENERGY REPORTS OIL DISCOVERY OFF ANGOLA IN BLOCK 15/06

BP PLC (L:BP.) Azule Energy Reports Oil Discovery Off Angola in Block 15/06. The Algaita-01 exploration well, located in the Lower Congo Basin, is estimated to hold nearly 500 million barrels of crude oil, according to a statement from Angola's National Oil, Gas, and Biofuels Agency (ANPG) on 26 Apr 2026. The discovery occurs within Azule Energy's ongoing campaign that has yielded 22 discoveries in Block 15/06 to date, highlighting the area's petroleum system. Drilling began January 10, 2026 at a water depth of 667 meters, approximately 18 kilometers from the Olombendo FPSO, and was completed on January 26. The Algaita-01 find reinforces BP's focus on expanding its African footprint, and adds to Azule Energy's portfolio in a region that ANPG has described as showing consistent exploration activity. Joe Murphy, CEO of Azule Energy, stated that the results reaffirm the high potential of the Lower Congo Basin. Paulino Jeronimo, Chairman & CEO of ANPG, emphasized the consistency of exploration in the area, underscoring support for continued activity in offshore Angola

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JGB-S5D3-RTDH-2467-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:47:55ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - THE RECENT INDUSTRY NEWS AND TRENDS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC.

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ MORTENSON DELIVERS CHI HEALTH GRAND ISLAND FAMILY HEALTH CENTER

Mortenson has delivered the new CHI Health Clinic Family Health Center in Grand Island, Nebraska, advancing access to comprehensive, coordinated care for communities across central Nebraska. The 40,165 squarefoot facility officially celebrated its grand opening on April 6 and brings primary care, specialty services, and essential support functions together in one centralized location-making care more convenient, efficient, and connected for patients and families.

Developed and built in close partnership with CommonSpirit Health and CHI Health, the Family Health Center represents a $30 million investment and is the first CommonSpirit (CHI) Family Health Center in Nebraska to offer full‑time specialty care. When fully staffed, the clinic is expected to support more than 500 patient visits per week and employ approximately 100 medical professionals.

Designed to support an integrated, patient focused care model, the facility consolidates services that were previously spread across multiple locations into a single destination for care. Offerings include priority care, primary care, internal medicine, and a broad range of specialty services, along with onsite lab and imaging, physical therapy, behavioral health, and space for a future retail pharmacy.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON COMPLETES USDA CAMPUS MODERNIZATION WITH STATE-OF-THE-ART BSL LABORATORIES

A vision of biosafety, functionality, and flexibility has been realized in a state-of-the-art biological safety level laboratory, encompassing BSL-2, ABSL-2, BSL-3E, ABSL-3E, and BSL-3AG spaces, delivered after seven years of meticulous planning, design, and phased construction. Awarded in 2017, this $149.2 million project involved the modernization of the Southeast Poultry Research Laboratory (SEPRL) campus for the USDA's National Poultry Research Center (NPRC) in Athens, Georgia. More than 3,200 workers logged 1.2 million hours to deliver one of the nation's most complex laboratory campuses, meeting USDA's evolving research needs with advanced biosafety and flexibility.

Established in 1962, the SEPRL research program focuses on exotic, emerging, and endemic viral poultry diseases with national and international impact. The modernization effort replaced outdated research facilities with state-of-the-art laboratories while tripling available research space to support the NPRC's mission of advancing poultry health, food safety, and global trade. Due to the critical nature of the research, it was imperative that the campus remain operational throughout construction, requiring extensive coordination with user groups to manage relocations to temporary and permanent facilities, minimizing disruption to ongoing research and operations.

Approximately 245,000 square feet of new research and administrative space was built as part of the project. The new 13-acre research campus was constructed within the footprint of the existing research buildings, requiring the development of an extensive phased construction approach to permit NPRC to continue mission-critical research during construction.

Source: Company Website

+ MORTENSON ADVANCES WOMEN IN CONSTRUCTION THROUGH WIC WEEK 2026

During Women in Construction Week (WIC), Mortenson moved beyond recognition to action-bringing teams, partners, and industry peers together to advance leadership, visibility, and opportunity for teams across the construction industry. Through industry events, project‑site engagement, and leadership storytelling, the week underscored how intentional investment in people strengthens teams, project outcomes, and the future workforce.

This year's WIC theme, "Level Up," focused on elevating how teams lead, support, and grow together. Across the organization, Mortenson highlighted the ways women strengthen project delivery, collaboration, and culture through leadership at every level.

Industry Events that Advance the Conversation The week kicked off with industry events designed to advance conversation and connection. In Minneapolis, employees and industry partners gathered to celebrate progress and reinforce the shared responsibility of shaping a more inclusive construction industry. The event featured a keynote from Emily Pilloton‑Lam, founder of Girls Garage, who explored how inclusive perspectives can reframe leadership, design, and long-term investment in people.

Source: Company Website

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [1 Press Release]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION LABOR SHORTAGE LOOMS AS CONSTRUCTION REBOUNDS

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION U.S. CONSTRUCTION DEMAND REMAINS STRONG AMID SHIFTING MARKET CONDITIONS

Turner Construction Company announced that the First Quarter 2026 Turner Building Cost Index-which measures costs in the non-residential building construction market in the United States-increased to the value of 1530. This represents a 1.32% quarterly increase from the Fourth Quarter 2025 and a 4.87% yearly increase from the First Quarter 2025.

"Demand across the U.S. construction market remains solid, though conditions vary by region and sector. High-growth areas such as data centers, manufacturing, and semiconductor facilities are driving strong demand, particularly in the Midwest and Southeast, while traditional commercial construction has softened in several markets. At the same time, architecture and engineering activity appears to be stabilizing. Material costs such as steel, aluminum, and copper have experienced increases during the first quarter," said Attilio Rivetti, the Turner vice president responsible for compiling the Cost Index. "We are closely monitoring rising oil prices, which could begin to impact transportation and material costs in the coming quarter."

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.
